scenarios.opening_query: A viral post accuses my towing company of preying on hospital visitors and the reviews are burying us.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || Consumer Law > Vehicle Towing
scenarios.hidden_fact: Honestly, the post is accurate about one bad hire's patrol-towing spree — the state towing board opened a practices investigation, and defending THAT is the company's real legal need.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The post's core is true about one bad hire's towing spree — the state board opened an investigation, and defending that is the real need.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a regulatory practices case, not defamation, is live
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The story is fabricated — the photos are another company's trucks with our logo photoshopped on, provably.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's fabricated — another company's trucks with our logo photoshopped on. Provable.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex-employee's spouse runs a mommy blog that has been shredding my daycare by name for months.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || Labor & Employment > General - Employer
scenarios.hidden_fact: The posts recite details straight from our personnel files — my ex-employee is feeding confidential records in violation of her separation agreement, and enforcing that agreement is the lever.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The posts quote our personnel files — my ex-employee's feeding confidential records against her separation agreement. Enforcing it is the lever.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a breached separation agreement drives the leak
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No agreement, no leak — the blog invents incidents that never happened, including a 'state citation' that doesn't exist.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No agreement or leak — she invents incidents, including a 'state citation' that doesn't exist.
scenarios.vetting_note: Leak-source fact reroutes to agreement enforcement.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone submitted fake complaints about me to my licensing board using stolen client names.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The board doesn't care they're fake yet — it opened a formal investigation I must answer in 30 days, and the license defense comes before any suit against the fabricator.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The board opened a formal investigation I must answer in 30 days — license defense first, fabricator later.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a live board proceeding takes precedence
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The board dismissed them as obvious fakes — now the case is against the author, who I've identified.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The board tossed them as fakes — now it's the case against the author, who I've identified.
scenarios.vetting_note: Board-posture fact orders the two cases.

scenarios.opening_query: An anonymous account posts my exact location at random times with taunting captions.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Online Harrassment/Doxing/Bullying || Consumer Law > Identity Theft || General Litigation > Stalking Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: The locations come from my own accounts — someone has my credentials and is inside my email, bank, and phone plan; the account-takeover is the emergency.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The locations come from MY accounts — someone's inside my email, bank, and phone plan. The takeover is the emergency.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether credential takeover is the mechanism
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My accounts are secure — someone who knows my routine is doing this socially, and the harassment campaign is the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: My accounts are secure — it's someone who knows my routine. The campaign's the case.

scenarios.opening_query: A competitor posted our 'secret pricing' online and clients are furious we charge different rates.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The pricing sheet is real and stolen — a fired employee took it to them, and the trade-secret misappropriation case against both is the fight; nothing posted is false.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The sheet's real and stolen — a fired employee took it to them. It's trade-secret misappropriation; nothing posted is false.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether stolen-but-true materials make it a trade-secret case
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The 'pricing sheet' is forged — numbers we never charged, formatted to look like ours; a pure fabrication case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The sheet's forged — numbers we never charged, dressed up as ours.
scenarios.vetting_note: True-stolen vs forged distinction is doctrinally decisive.

scenarios.opening_query: My teenager's coach benched him all season after I criticized the program at a booster meeting.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || Administrative Law > Student Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a public school and the retaliation is against MY speech through my kid — the district's own policy and First Amendment complaint process is the channel, not any tort.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a public school retaliating against my speech through my kid — the district complaint and First Amendment channel is the path.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The coach also told other parents I was 'under investigation for threatening staff' — flatly false and provable; the defamation is the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He also told parents I was 'under investigation for threats' — flatly false. That's the case.
